task.title: Setting the default gateway on an existing connection by using nm-connection-editor

li.p: At least one static IP address must be configured on the connection on which the default gateway will be set.

context.p: In most situations, administrators set the default gateway when they create a connection. However, you can also set or update the default gateway setting on a previously created connection using the nm-connection-editor application.

step.cmd: Open a terminal, and enter nm-connection-editor:
Select the connection to modify, and click the gear wheel icon to edit the existing connection.
Set the IPv4 default gateway. For example, to set the IPv4 address of the default gateway on the connection to 192.0.2.1:

li.p: Enter the address in the gateway field next to the IP range the gateway’s address is within:

step.cmd: Set the IPv6 default gateway. For example, to set the IPv6 address of the default gateway on the connection to 2001:db8:1::1:

li.p: Enter the address in the gateway field next to the IP range the gateway’s address is within:

step.cmd: Restart the network connection for changes to take effect. For example, to restart the example connection using the command line:

note.p: All connections currently using this network connection are temporarily interrupted during the restart.

li.p: Verify that the route is active.
To display the IPv4 default gateway:
li.codeblock: # ip -4 route default via 192.0.2.1 dev example proto static metric 100
li.p: To display the IPv6 default gateway:
li.codeblock: # ip -6 route default via 2001:db8:1::1 dev example proto static metric 100 pref medium

p.xref: Configuring an Ethernet connection by using nm-connection-editor
